method: attentive_probe.evaluate: small regularized MLPClassifier head (hidden=(32,), alpha=1.0) vs linear probe, both through probe.auc_ci_perm (StandardScaler + in-fold auto-PCA, site-disjoint CV, bootstrap CI, permutation null, repeated-CV ensembling). Grounding = leave-one-group-out AUC attribution over embedding + named markers.
paper_reference: NeuroVFM, Nat Med 2026 (s41591-026-04497-1): frozen Vol-JEPA encoder (5.24M volumes) + attentive MLP probe for downstream diagnosis. We adapt honestly to a pooled 768-d embedding at n in the hundreds: report (not assume) nonlinear lift; LOGO attribution substitutes for spatial attention maps our pooled embedding cannot provide.

evaluate.verdict: MLP head matches the linear probe (delta=-0.0015) — the frozen embedding is already near-linearly separable at this n

feature_grounding.note: leave-one-group-out AUC attribution over the frozen embedding + named clinical features (interpretable grounding). Attribution, NOT a learned spatial attention map — the pooled NeuroJEPA embedding has no per-region tokens.

evaluate.verdict: MLP head does not beat linear (delta=-0.0683) — nonlinearity does not pay off at this n; prefer the simpler linear head

feature_grounding.note: leave-one-group-out AUC attribution over the frozen embedding + named clinical features (interpretable grounding). Attribution, NOT a learned spatial attention map — the pooled NeuroJEPA embedding has no per-region tokens.

evaluate.verdict: MLP head does not beat linear (delta=-0.0218) — nonlinearity does not pay off at this n; prefer the simpler linear head

feature_grounding.note: leave-one-group-out AUC attribution over the frozen embedding + named clinical features (interpretable grounding). Attribution, NOT a learned spatial attention map — the pooled NeuroJEPA embedding has no per-region tokens.

compliance: Frozen inference only; weights never stored; embedding tables git-ignored. Only AUC-grade numbers written here.
